title: malus communis subsp. pumila
intro: Malus communis subsp. pumila, also known as the dwarf crabapple or rock crabapple, is a subspecies of the wild apple tree that belongs to the Rosaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Malus communis subsp. pumila include reducing fever, relieving cough and sore throat, and providing relief from digestive issues. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and tinctures, can be made from its leaves, flowers, and fruits, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
